title: RNA extraction with trizol for tissues
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3of2r","text":"Clean the benches and all the material that will be used with alcohol 70. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cfnnl","text":"Use tips with filter. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eshkn","text":"Cool the centrifuge to 4°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 1 mL of Trizol to amounts of 50 to 100 mg and homogenize with the homogenizer. Pass to a tube. In this step, you can freeze this sample for up to 6 months in the -80C freezer or continue the protocol.
Incubate for 5 minutes to permit complete dissociation of the nucleoproteins complex.
Add 200 μl L of chloroform per 1 mL of TRIzol and incubate for 2 to 3 minutes.
Centrifuge the sample for 15 minutes at 12,000 × g at 4°C.

The mixture separates into a lower red phenol-chloroform, and interphase, and a colorless upper aqueous phase. Transfer the aqueous (incolor) phase containing the RNA to a new tube by angling the tube at 45° and pipetting the solution out.
Add 500 μl of isopropanol per 1 mL of TRIzol. Incubate for 10 minutes.
Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 12.000 × g at 4°C.
Discard the supernatant with a micropipetto and resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of 75% ethanol per 1 mL of TRIzol.
Vortex the sample briefly and centrifuge for 5 minutes at 7.500 × g at 4°C.
Discard the supernatant with a micropipettor. Vacuum or air dry the RNA pellet for 5–10 minutes.

Resuspend the pellet in 20–50 µL of RNase-free water, 0.1 mM EDTA, or 0.5% SDS solution by pipetting up and down. Note: Do not dissolve the RNA in 0.5% SDS if the RNA is to be used in subsequent enzymatic reactions.
Incubate in a water bath or heat block set at 55–60°C for 10–15 minutes. Then store in -80°C freezer.
creator: Maysa Silva
